title: Tax Loss Harvesting - Long-Term Capital Losses

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must strategically sell securities held over 12 months at a loss to offset long-term capital gains and up to $3,000 ordinary income while maintaining desired portfolio allocation and avoiding wash sale rules
criteria: Identify positions held over 12 months trading below cost basis with unrealized long-term capital losses
Sell losing positions to realize losses for current tax year deduction
Long-term capital losses first offset long-term capital gains, then short-term gains, then up to $3,000 ordinary income
Excess losses carry forward indefinitely to future tax years maintaining character as long-term losses
Avoid wash sale rule by not repurchasing substantially identical security within 61-day window (30 days before/after)
Can immediately repurchase similar but not substantially identical security to maintain market exposure
Harvest losses annually, especially in years with large capital gains or year-end portfolio rebalancing
Document cost basis, holding periods, and wash sale analysis for accurate tax reporting

ltcl_harvesting_strategy.loss_ordering: LTCL offsets LTCG first, then STCG, then $3,000 ordinary income; excess carries forward
ltcl_harvesting_strategy.carryforward_unlimited: Unused losses carry forward indefinitely maintaining long-term character
ltcl_harvesting_strategy.portfolio_maintenance: Replace with similar ETF or mutual fund to maintain allocation without triggering wash sale
ltcl_harvesting_strategy.tax_bracket_benefit: Saves 0-20% on offset LTCG plus 3.8% NIIT if applicable, or marginal rate on $3,000 ordinary
ltcl_harvesting_strategy.basis_reset: Repurchasing similar position resets basis lower potentially creating future gains
ltcl_harvesting_strategy.annual_strategy: Year-end review identifies harvest opportunities; can harvest throughout year

restrictions: Wash sale rule disallows loss if repurchase substantially identical security within 61-day window
Wash sale applies to purchases in IRAs, spouse's accounts, and controlled entities
Substantially identical securities include same issuer stock, not typically different companies even in same sector
ETF swap opportunities exist (SPY to VOO) but must analyze substantial identity risk
Options, short sales, and futures on same security within wash window trigger wash sale rule
Cannot deduct losses on sales to related parties under §267 related party loss disallowance
State tax treatment generally follows federal but verify state conformity to federal loss rules
Must track basis, wash sales, and holding periods accurately; brokers report but taxpayer ultimately responsible
